data.title: Financial news roundup: Vanguard's 2026 outlook, Saviynt's $700 million funding, PNC's new "crypto for clients" service.
data.description: This week, notable financial announcements included Vanguard's release of the 2026 economic outlook. Saviynt secured a substantial $700 million in funding, while PNC introduced a new "crypto for clients" solution. The information was shared via PR Newswire in New York on December 12, 2025. These updates were among the many press releases that circulated this week.
EU agrees three-euro import tax on small parcels from July 2026

data.title: Bitcoin's hashrate is at record levels in 2026 but miner rewards and revenues are decreasing. Investors must tie Bitcoin income to network security. Previous income strategies failed when market conditions changed. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: As 2026 approaches, there is a growing disparity between the security of the Bitcoin network and its market value. The network has achieved record-level security with a high hashrate, but the economics of mining are increasingly challenging. Miner rewards are decreasing, difficulty levels are rising, and revenue per unit of compute has become minimal, leaving miners struggling to cover basic costs. Similar trends were observed in the Grayscale Bitcoin Trust in 2021, where premiums collapsed and liquidity dwindled. To avoid a similar cycle, Bitcoin income should be linked to the network's security. Previous market phases have shown that relying on financial structures tied to perfect conditions is unsustainable. Popular Bitcoin income strategies based on premium-based trades, high-yield lenders, or token-incentive programs faced difficulties when conditions changed. To ensure sustainable Bitcoin income, it must be linked to the network's security rather than temporary financial arrangements.
Germany accuses Russia of a cyberattack against aviation security and election interference

data.title: Highlights of consumer press releases this week: Dove, Crumbl serve seconds; eos in fragrance mist; Hallmark's Disney love story.
data.description: This week, several notable consumer and retail announcements were made through PR Newswire. Highlights include Dove and Crumbl introducing new offerings with additional servings, eos expanding into the fragrance mist sector, and Hallmark unveiling a Disney-themed love story. These updates were part of a series of press releases issued from various companies, signaling significant developments in the industry. The news came amidst a bustling week in New York, where PR Newswire headquarters is located, on December 12, 2025.

data.title: Phantom and Kalshi partner to bring prediction markets to a leading crypto wallet, offering users real-time trading options.
data.description: In San Francisco, Phantom and Kalshi have teamed up to launch a new feature called Phantom Prediction Markets. This innovative platform allows users to engage in trading on trending topics such as politics, cryptocurrency, sports, and culture, all within the Phantom crypto wallet. With over 20 million users, Phantom is a trusted name in the industry. Users can easily open positions in these markets by trading tokens. Kalshi, known as the world's largest prediction market, boasts record volumes. This collaboration provides an accessible way for users to participate in real-time market movements directly from their wallet.
Defense forces liberated Kindrashivka and Radivka, stabilizing the situation on the Kupiansk direction

data.title: Clearmind Medicine Inc. has regained compliance with Nasdaq's Minimum Stockholders' Equity Rule of $2,500,000.
data.description: Clearmind Medicine Inc., a biotech company based in Vancouver, Canada, revealed today that they have met Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(b)(1) by maintaining stockholders’ equity of at least $2,500,000. The notification letter from Nasdaq, based on the Company’s recent Form 6-K filing, confirmed their compliance on December 10, 2025. Clearmind focuses on developing innovative neuroplastogen-derived therapeutics to address untreated health issues, marking a significant milestone in their clinical-stage research endeavors.

data.title: DTCC tokenization approved, SEC Chair Paul Atkins promotes onchain settlement for US financial markets.
data.description: On Thursday, SEC Chair Paul Atkins reaffirmed his support for tokenizing US financial markets and utilizing onchain settlement. Atkins emphasized his vision during comments, highlighting the potential benefits of incorporating blockchain technology in the financial sector. This move could revolutionize the traditional financial markets by introducing more efficient processes and increased transparency. By advocating for this approach, Atkins aims to embrace innovation and modernize the financial industry for the future. His commitment to exploring tokenization and onchain settlement signifies a significant shift towards digital transformation within the sector, signaling a pivotal moment.
